title: Complexity Theory of Randomised Testing
authors: Pingshi Yu, Chengsong Tan, Nicolas Wu, Alastair Donaldson
abstract: Randomised testing is a widely-used approach to software validation, yet its theoretical foundations remain thin. In particular, the fundamental question of what it means for a set of inputs to be \emph{generable} has gone unanswered in both the literature and folklore. We present the first complexity-theoretic foundations for random generators in software testing. We model generators as Turing transducers that consume random bits and produce string-encoded outputs, and show that the theoretically generable languages coincide exactly with the recursively enumerable languages. This has direct implications for testing at the boundaries of decidability, such as compiler testing. For \emph{efficient} generation, we show that the polynomial-time generable languages lie within \textit{NP}, that certain \textit{NP}-complete languages admit efficient generators, and that -- under standard cryptographic assumptions -- there are languages in \textit{P} for which no efficient generator exists: the complexity of efficienct generation and of efficient decision are not the same. We show space-bounded complexity is the natural framework for generators producing \emph{correlated} samples, capturing methodologies such as coverage-guided fuzzing and symbolic execution. Beyond classification, we characterise efficient generability: a language has a polynomial-time generator iff it admits a \emph{certificate scheme} over a verifier -- so witness planting, the folklore technique behind generators to test SAT solvers, is in a sense the only route to efficient generation. On the design of property-based testing libraries, we prove no library can compositionally derive efficient generators from logical predicates involving conjunction or negation, under standard assumptions. However, restricted classes like \textit{NL} (equivalently, linear Datalog predicates) would admit such a compilation.
title_zh: 随机测试的复杂性理论
abstract_zh: 随机测试是一种广泛采用的软件验证方法，但其理论基础仍显薄弱。特别地，关于一组输入具备\emph{可生成性}究竟意味着什么这一根本问题，无论在文献还是行业惯例中都悬而未决。我们为软件测试中的随机生成器提出了首个基于计算复杂度的理论基础。我们将生成器建模为消耗随机比特并输出字符串编码结果的图灵转换器，并证明：理论上可生成的语言恰好与递归可枚举语言相重合。这一结论对诸如编译器测试等处于可判定边界上的测试实践具有直接影响。在\emph{高效生成}方面，我们证明多项式时间可生成的语言包含于\textit{NP}类，某些\textit{NP}完全语言存在高效生成器，并且——在标准密码学假设下——\textit{P}类中存在某些语言没有高效生成器：高效生成与高效判定的复杂度并不相同。我们证明空间有界复杂度是适用于生成\emph{关联样本}的生成器的自然框架，它能够刻画覆盖导向模糊测试和符号执行等方法论。除分类之外，我们还刻画了高效可生成性：一个语言具有多项式时间生成器，当且仅当它在一个验证器上存在\emph{凭证方案}——因此，作为测试SAT求解器的生成器背后的惯用技术，见证植入在某种意义上正是实现高效生成的唯一途径。对于基于属性的测试库的设计，我们证明在标准假设下，没有任何测试库能够从涉及合取或否定的逻辑谓词中以组合方式推导出高效生成器。然而，像\textit{NL}（等价于线性Datalog谓词）这样的受限类则有可能支持此类编译。

title: Knowledge-Guided Synthetic Bug Feedback for LLM-Based Unit Test Generation
authors: Ziheng Wang, Maike Li, Chen Zhi
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have opened new opportunities for unit test generation, but executable tests do not necessarily reveal real defects. This paper studies how historical real-bug mechanisms can be transformed into executable feedback targets for LLM-based unit test generation. The proposed framework constructs structural and semantic representations of real-bug records, retrieves mechanisms applicable to a focal method, and instantiates them as synthetic bugs that guide iterative test enhancement. We evaluate the approach on method-level real-bug detection tasks from Defects4J and show that mechanism-guided synthetic-bug feedback improves real-bug detection over execution-, coverage-, mutation-, knowledge-, and search-based baselines. The results suggest that organizing real-bug mechanisms as retrievable and executable feedback targets is an effective way to guide generated tests toward bug-triggering inputs and behavioral oracles.
title_zh: 面向基于大语言模型的单元测试生成的知识引导合成缺陷反馈
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）为单位测试生成开辟了新机遇，但可执行的测试未必能揭示真实缺陷。本文研究如何将历史上的真实缺陷机制转化为基于LLM的单元测试生成的可执行反馈目标。所提出的框架构建了真实缺陷记录的结构化和语义化表示，检索适用于目标方法的机制，并将其实例化为引导迭代测试增强的合成缺陷。我们在Defects4J的方法级真实缺陷检测任务上评估了该方法，结果表明，机制引导的合成缺陷反馈在真实缺陷检测上优于基于执行、覆盖、变异、知识和搜索的基线方法。这一结果表明，将真实缺陷机制组织为可检索且可执行的反馈目标，是引导所生成的测试向缺陷触发输入及行为断言方向发展的有效途径。

title: BackendForge: Benchmarking Agentic End-to-End Code Generation with Backend Services
authors: Yuzhe Guo, Mengzhou Wu, Yuan Cao, Jialei Wei, Dezhi Ran, Wei Yang, Tao Xie
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in agentic coding settings, where they can inspect files, execute commands, run tests, observe failures, and iteratively revise code. This shift raises a central evaluation question: can an agentic LLM generate an end-to-end software artifact that is both deployable and behaviorally correct under execution? Backend services provide a controlled but realistic substrate for this evaluation. Their APIs expose application-level executable semantics, and deployed behavior can be checked deterministically against an OpenAPI contract through black-box HTTP interactions. We introduce BackendForge, a benchmark of 56 contract-defined backend generation tasks rewritten from real open-source applications. Given a visible specification and an OpenAPI contract, an LLM must generate a Dockerized service that is built, deployed, and evaluated only through HTTP tests. To strengthen evaluation without introducing hidden requirements, BackendForge uses a test agent and a code agent to co-evolve the test oracle and reference service, where the test agent proposes specification-grounded backend tests and the code agent repairs the reference implementation. Although the best-performing model, GPT-5.5, succeeds on 55.4\% of tasks under the base oracle, it succeeds on only 28.6\% under the final oracle. This gap suggests that current LLMs can implement many local API behaviors, but still struggle to produce complete backend services.
title_zh: BackendForge：基于后端服务的智能体端到端代码生成基准测试
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地被用于智能体式编码环境中，在这些环境中，它们能够检查文件、执行命令、运行测试、观察失败并迭代修改代码。这一转变引出一个核心评估问题：智能体式LLM能否生成一个端到端的、既可部署又在执行时行为正确的软件产物？后端服务为此评估提供了一个可控但真实的基底，其API暴露了应用层可执行语义，部署后的行为可通过黑盒HTTP交互，依据OpenAPI契约进行确定性检查。我们引入了BackendForge，这是一个包含56个基于契约定义的后端生成任务的基准测试，这些任务改写自真实开源应用。给定可视规范与OpenAPI契约，LLM必须生成一个Docker化服务，该服务仅通过HTTP测试进行构建、部署和评估。为在不引入隐藏需求的条件下强化评估，BackendForge采用一个测试智能体与一个代码智能体，共同演化测试预言和参考服务：测试智能体提出基于规范的后端测试，代码智能体修复参考实现。尽管表现最优的模型GPT-5.5在基础预言下成功完成了55.4%的任务，但在最终预言下仅成功28.6%。这一差距表明，当前的LLM可以完成许多局部API行为，但生成完整的后端服务仍然面临困难。

title: Fail-Aware and Explainable Test Oracle Prediction
authors: Yue Zhao, Binish Tanveer, Jelena Zdravkovic
abstract: Despite their central role in fault detection, test oracles remain challenging to construct effectively. Recent learning based methods address this challenge by automatically generating test assertions, yet even if syntactically correct, they are often ineffective in revealing bugs. Rather than generating assertions, this study explores a different approach by training a model to directly predict whether a given test prefix passes or fails. We present FOCAL, an emerging code LLM-based discriminative oracle predictor. It learns from labeled pairs of test prefixes and methods under test, employs losses that emphasize failing cases during training, and grounds its predictions in statement level behavioral evidence. Compared with the baseline method SEER, we substantially improve performance on failing cases for unseen projects and provide richer explanations. A preliminary evaluation on fault-detection benchmarks and automated test-generation artifacts shows that our approach is highly accurate within its training distribution and substantially improves failure detection on previously unseen projects where prior discriminative oracles collapse. Moreover, the highlighted statements are supported by behavioral explanation checks. These early results suggest that fail-aware discriminative oracle prediction can complement existing approaches such as fuzzing, search-based testing, and LLM-based test generation. These techniques produce test prefixes at scale but often lack fault oriented oracles. In future work, FOCAL could take generated test prefixes and attach fault-aware predicted oracles to them, turning high-volume input generation into executable tests that are more likely to expose semantic failures.
title_zh: 失效感知且可解释的测试预言预测
abstract_zh: 尽管测试预言在故障检测中扮演着核心角色，但有效构建它们仍然充满挑战。近年来，基于学习的方法通过自动生成测试断言来应对这一挑战，然而即使语法正确，这些断言在揭示错误方面往往效果不佳。本研究另辟蹊径，不生成断言，而是训练模型直接预测给定测试前缀是通过还是失败。我们提出了FOCAL，这是一种基于代码大语言模型的新兴判别式预言预测器。它从带标签的测试前缀与被测方法对中学习，在训练过程中采用强调失败案例的损失函数，并将预测建立在语句级行为证据之上。与基线方法SEER相比，我们大幅提升了对未见项目中失败案例的检测性能，并提供了更丰富的解释。在故障检测基准和自动化测试生成工件上的初步评估表明，我们的方法在其训练分布内高度准确，并且在之前判别式预言失效的未见项目上显著改善了故障检测。此外，高亮语句得到了行为解释检查的支持。这些早期结果提示，对失败案例敏感的判别式预言预测可以补强模糊测试、基于搜索的测试以及基于大语言模型的测试生成等现有方法。这些技术能大规模生成测试前缀，但往往缺乏面向错误的预言。在未来的工作中，FOCAL可以将生成的测试前缀赋予故障感知的预测预言，从而将大批量的输入生成转化为更有可能暴露语义故障的可执行测试。
